destinations.evidence: RPC handlers store the standard EthPubSub directly and construct it with EthPubSub::new; searches found no UniversalEthPubSub, OptimizedEthPubSub, RETH_OPTIMIZED_PUBSUB, or new_optimized path in the local port.

equivalence_argument: Because the handler type is EthPubSub<EthApi> and construction is direct, no optimized pubsub environment selection can alter the implementation.
notes: The optimized pubsub symbols are absent from the audited port.
rationale: The merge deletes the UniversalEthPubSub enum and optimized pubsub bootstrap paths, changes EthHandlers.pubsub to EthPubSub<EthApi>, constructs EthPubSub::with_spawner directly, and switches registry construction from new_optimized to new. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: EthHandlers must hold and bootstrap the standard EthPubSub handler directly; RETH_OPTIMIZED_PUBSUB and UniversalEthPubSub/OptimizedEthPubSub selection must not change the pubsub implementation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set RETH_OPTIMIZED_PUBSUB=true and bootstrap RPC handlers; assert the resulting pubsub handler is the standard EthPubSub and no optimized routing implementation is instantiated.
summary: RPC builder uses the standard EthPubSub handler.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RETH_OPTIMIZED_PUBSUB=true and bootstrap RPC handlers; assert EthHandlers.pubsub is still the standard EthPubSub path.
title: RPC builder uses standard pubsub
